prompt: Healthcare spending as percentage of GDP
resolvedPrompt: Map healthcare spending as a percentage of GDP by country

questions.question: Which countries or regions should the map cover?
Do you have this data or can you provide a GeoJSON/CSV file?
options: I have a file to upload

dataWarning: Healthcare spending as % of GDP is not available in Atlas's built-in datasets. This metric is typically sourced from OECD Health Statistics, World Bank, or WHO. You'll need to: (1) download the data from OECD.org, data.worldbank.org, or WHO GHO, (2) convert to GeoJSON format with country identifiers, (3) upload to Atlas. Alternatively, contact your data provider to request a pre-formatted geographic dataset.

questions.question: Visualize median income for: municipalities in Stockholm, or the whole county?
Do you have access to Swedish income data (SCB, Statistics Sweden)?
options: Yes, I have a CSV/GeoJSON file

dataWarning: Median income data for Swedish regions is typically available from Statistics Sweden (SCB) but not exposed through standard public APIs. You may need to download data from scb.se or provide a CSV/GeoJSON file with income values per municipality to map it in Atlas.

resolvedPrompt: Map of immigrants by municipality (kommune) in Norway with population counts or percentages

questions.question: Should the map show absolute numbers or percentage of immigrants per municipality?
Do you have access to Statistics Norway (SSB) data or can you upload a CSV?
options: I have SSB data to upload
I can provide a data file

dataWarning: Immigrant statistics by Norwegian municipality are not available in public APIs. This data is typically available directly from Statistics Norway (SSB) at data.ssb.no. You can download the dataset and upload it as CSV or GeoJSON to Atlas. Recommend querying table 05196 (population by immigration background and municipality) or similar from SSB's data portal.

expectedIntent: Real-time weather — likely no good source

questions.question: What weather data do you need: current conditions, forecast, historical patterns, or climate normals?

dataWarning: Atlas is a mapping platform for geographic data (boundaries, events, amenities). Real-time weather requires a dedicated weather API (e.g. OpenWeatherMap, WeatherAPI). Consider: Do you want to map Stockholm on a basemap, or visualize weather patterns across regions?
